reforms.description: The Turkish government approved an SME Strategy in November 2003, which includes a short-term Action Plan for 2004-2006 aimed at strengthening research and development infrastructure and promoting local technological clusters. Additionally, several schemes subsidise target research-development, training, and technology acquisition costs at very high rates of 70-90%, although the programme budgets are restricted to a small number of beneficiaries, raising competition issues.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The reforms aim to enhance R&D capabilities and support local technological development, which are expected to boost productivity and innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The SME Strategy was approved and the subsidy schemes were initiated as part of the government's efforts to enhance R&D.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reforms represent significant structural changes aimed at enhancing the R&D landscape and supporting SMEs.
reforms.source_quote: Several schemes subsidise target (eligible) research-development, training, technology acquisition etc. costs at very high rates of 70-90 per cent.

reforms.description: The Turkish government has initiated a critical review of the public education system, focusing on modernizing curricula, increasing teacher quality, and equipping schools with new technologies.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving education quality and equipping schools with new technologies is expected to enhance the skills of the workforce, thereby supporting innovation and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The review and modernization efforts have been officially initiated and are in progress.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant overhaul of the education system, which is crucial for building human capital.
reforms.source_quote: The Ministry of Education declared in summer 2004 that a critical review is focusing on six main areas: i) Increasing effective enrollment rates; ii) modernizing the curricula; iii) increasing the quality of teachers; iv) equipping schools with new technologies.

reforms.description: The Public Financial Management and Control Law mandates the introduction of multi-year functional budgeting, which aims to enhance transparency and efficiency in public spending by aligning budgets with strategic objectives and performance indicators.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing transparency and efficiency in public spending is expected to improve resource allocation for innovation and R&D.
reforms.status_evidence: The law has been passed and is awaiting implementation.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant change in how public finances are managed, impacting various sectors including innovation.
reforms.source_quote: The Public Financial Management and Control Law prescribes the introduction of functional budgeting in all areas of public spending.

reforms.description: The Public Administration Framework Law was passed by Parliament in June 2004, aiming to transform public administration in Turkey by decentralizing responsibilities and enhancing accountability in service delivery.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Decentralizing responsibilities and enhancing accountability can lead to more efficient public service delivery, indirectly supporting innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The law has been passed and is in effect.
reforms.importance_rationale: This law represents a significant structural change in public administration.
reforms.source_quote: The Public Administration Framework Law was passed by Parliament in June 2004.
